mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for two consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance increases real borrowing costs and reduces equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 5.33% indicates restrictive cycle
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, reducing corporate earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of months following PAYEMS declines, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 60-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When real retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month while the unemployment rate rises simultaneously, consumer demand contraction combined with labor market weakness signals aggregate demand collapse, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines MoM AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in same month
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 697557.0 million dollars, UNRATE at 3.8%
failure_condition: Joint signal (RSAFS decline + UNRATE rise) produces negative 20-day returns in < 55% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while remaining positive, signaling supply-side weakness and margin compression that reduces forward equity returns over the next 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances when INDPRO growth is 0-0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above 1.3 million annualized units while real retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously grow month-over-month, indicating synchronized demand strength across residential and consumer sectors that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1.3M annualized AND RSAFS grows MoM
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1283.0 thousand units, RSAFS at 697557.0 million dollars
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 60-day returns in < 58% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) decelerates to below 3.0% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains above 4.5%, the real interest rate becomes restrictively high, signaling monetary policy overshoot that constrains equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL at 307.481 index, FEDFUNDS at 5.33%
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in < 55% of triggered instances, or real rate overshoot does not explain incremental variance beyond univariate FEDFUNDS effect (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) remains below 4.0% while nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) grows at least 150,000 persons month-over-month, indicating tight labor market conditions that support consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4.0% AND PAYEMS MoM growth > 150K persons
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 3.8%, PAYEMS at 156874.0 thousand
regime_hint: tight labor market with strong employment growth
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 60-day returns in < 58% of triggered instances, or interaction term does not improve model fit over univariate labor indicators (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth decelerates to below 2.0% annualized in the most recent quarter, signaling late-cycle slowdown and reduced earnings growth expectations that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors reprice terminal growth rates.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 22225.35 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of quarters following sub-2.0% GDP growth, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.
